banner: Week 1 · Meet Your Tools · Start the Compass
essential_question: What does it take to draw geometry you can trust?
objective: Identify and safely use the compass, straightedge, and protractor, and start The Salas Compass as your running notebook.

content.prompt: Three days a week we meet: Concept, Build, Prove. In between, the Math-U-See work is yours. What do you already know about geometry — and what do you want to build with it?

content.heading: Four tools, one job each
bullets: Straightedge — draws a straight line (never measure with its markings during a construction).
Compass — draws circles and equal lengths; the width IS the length.
Protractor — measures and marks angles in degrees.
Pencil — sharp, light; geometry is done in pencil so you can fix it.

content.heading: Your notebook is a tool too
content.note: Label the first page 'The Salas Compass — Geometry.' This is where your before-session reads, sketches, and build photos live all year. Draw today's date and write one goal for the year.

content.heading: Draw the four you'll master this week

content.preview: Next session: FreeCAD + measuring the real world.

success_criteria.text: I can name the compass, straightedge, and protractor and what each is for
I have started The Salas Compass notebook

questions.prompt: Match the job to the tool: (a) draw a perfect circle, (b) draw a straight line, (c) measure a 60° angle.
questions.explain: Compass = circles/equal lengths; straightedge = straight lines; protractor = angles.

questions.prompt: Freehand-draw and label a point, a segment, a ray, and a line. (We make them precise this week.)
questions.answer: Four labeled figures: a dot, a two-end segment, a one-arrow ray, a two-arrow line.
questions.explain: Rubric: all four present and labeled; precision not required today.

questions.prompt: Exit: write the one goal you set on the first page of your Compass.
